records.ety: [L. patens, -entis, p. pr. of patere to be open: cf. F. patent. Cf. Fathom.]

defs: Open; expanded; evident; apparent; unconcealed; manifest; public; conspicuous.
quotes: He had received instructions, both patent and secret.

defs: Open to public perusal; -- said of a document conferring some right or privilege; as, letters patent. See Letters patent, under 3d Letter.
Appropriated or protected by letters patent; secured by official authority to the exclusive possession, control, and disposal of some person or party; patented; as, a patent right; patent medicines.
quotes: Madder . . . in King Charles the First's time, was made a patent commodity.

defs: Spreading; forming a nearly right angle with the steam or branch; as, a patent leaf.
A letter patent, or letters patent; an official document, issued by a sovereign power, conferring a right or privilege on some person or party.
A writing securing to an invention.
A document making a grant and conveyance of public lands.
quotes: Four other gentlemen of quality remained mentioned in that patent.
notes: ☞ In the United States, by the act of 1870, patents for inventions are issued for seventeen years, without the privilege of renewal except by act of Congress.

defs: The right or privilege conferred by such a document; hence, figuratively, a right, privilege, or license of the nature of a patent.
quotes: If you are so fond over her iniquity, give her patent to offend.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Patented; p. pr. & vb. n. Patenting.]

defs: To grant by patent; to make the subject of a patent; to secure or protect by patent; as, to patent an invention; to patent public lands.
